tweets.text:
Is your job at AI risk? 

While studies agree on low-risk jobs (like dancers), they disagree wildly on high-risk ones (like tax preparers).

The jobs labeled most "at risk" are actually the ones we understand least.

Torsten at Apollo https://t.co/bz19IF7ppu

tweets.text:
Chile’s copper production continues to weaken, with output declining across its three largest mines in May. 

That only adds to an already tight supply backdrop as demand from grid expansion, electrification, & AI infrastructure keeps growing. 

And despite the recent correction across metals, copper has continued to hold near its highs, & once momentum turns, it’s not going to take much for prices to move meaningfully higher.
tweets.full_text:
Chile’s copper production continues to weaken, with output declining across its three largest mines in May. 

That only adds to an already tight supply backdrop as demand from grid expansion, electrification, & AI infrastructure keeps growing. 

And despite the recent correction across metals, copper has continued to hold near its highs, & once momentum turns, it’s not going to take much for prices to move meaningfully higher.

What if the biggest advantage in today's markets isn't making more trades or winning every debate—but knowing when to step back?
